Skill in surviving as a Hardcore player. You earn XP in this skill by killing dangerous creatures while Hardcore. (The creatures' level must be higher than your Hardcore Living skill.)

skill_HolisticWellness_Description: An approach to health that involves taking into account factors beyond the obvious. Some critics claim that any benefits are due to the placebo effect, and they may be right. But if it works, who cares?
skill_Hoplology_Description:
The study of weapons and armor, with a focus on the benefits and weaknesses of different kinds of equipment.

As the skill increases, Staff users will find that their combat abilities also restore Armor.
skill_Howling_Description: Skill in howling as a werewolf.
skill_IceConjuration_Description: A sub-skill of Ice Magic dedicated to the creation of delicate ice items. These items tend to melt in hot environments.

skill_MagicalRoasting_Description:
Techniques to roast creatures alive with magic. Often considered horrific and immoral, but the results are inarguably delicious.

For best results, start with weak creatures and work your way up as your skill improves. Don't use huge single blasts -- hit the target with as many different Fire damage instances as possible to increase cooking likelihood. And most importantly: their last Hit Point must be lost to Fire damage (either direct or indirect).

skill_Meditation_Description:

To use necromantic abilities, you must be wielding or wearing a necromancy gem. You can often find these on weapons, and less often on pieces of armor. (If you are in a beast form, necromancy gems in weapons won't work, but ones in armor will.) You must NOT be wearing a symbol of Arisetsu, as it will negate your powers.

skill_Orcish_Description: Fluency in Orcish, the ancient language of orcs -- as well as many other races and cultures.

You must be wearing a holy symbol -- and NOT wearing a Necromancy Gem -- to use Priest abilities. Holy symbols are most often found as jewelry, but can also be found on armor and weapons.
